title: Intensely Smoky Chinese Masarepa Bao
description: A fresh and comforting Chinese dish featuring white masarepa, with a smoky and spicy flavor, perfect for those looking for an egg-free friendly option.
instructions: In a large bowl, mix together the white masarepa, smoked paprika, hot sauce, and salt until well combined.
Gradually add in the sesame oil, stirring with a fork until the mixture forms a crumbly dough.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and pliable.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a disk shape and place a few chopped scallions and grated ginger in the center of each disk.
Fold the dough over the filling to form a half-moon shape and press the edges together to seal the bao.
Place the bao on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving about 1 inch of space between each bao.
Bake the bao in a preheated oven at 400 degrees Fahrenheit for 20 minutes, or until golden brown.
Remove the bao from the oven and let them cool for a few minutes before serving.
Serve the bao warm with your favorite dipping sauce.
